baseline_reference.title: OECD Model Tax Convention 2017 baseline reference for Articles 10 to 12

delta_analysis.summary: The bilateral treaty has a 10% general rate for dividends, compared to the OECD baseline of 15%. The reduced rate for direct corporate holdings is 5% in both, but the bilateral treaty lacks the 365-day holding period condition.

The bilateral treaty includes an exemption for interest derived by governmental entities and related debt-claims, which is not present in the OECD baseline. The general withholding rate of 10% matches the baseline.
The bilateral treaty allows source-state taxation of royalties with a 10% cap, whereas the OECD baseline provides exclusive residence-state taxation (0% source-state rate).
